emails.subject: Tax season - a couple things to think about
emails.content:
Hi Daniel,

Just a heads-up as you work on your taxes this year. A few things to think about:

- If you apply your tax refund directly to the Capital One balance, it'll make a real dent. Even $500-800 toward the principal saves you $150+ in interest over the next year.
- Your 1099-K from Stripe means you'll report self-employment income. Make sure you're tracking business expenses (parts, shipping, packaging) since those reduce your taxable amount.
- Self-employment tax (15.3%) applies on top of income tax for that portion. The deductions help offset this.
- If you're filing jointly, all income sources will appear on the return. Let me know if you'd like to talk through how to approach that before you sit down to file.

You're making good progress. Your credit score is up 14 points since we started working together. Let's keep the momentum going.

Want to schedule a call this week or next?

Best,
Lisa Brennan
Credit Counselor

lbrennan@clearpathcredit.org

emails.subject: Re: March check-in — great progress!
emails.content:
Hi Lisa,

Sorry for the slow reply — I've been doing a deep dive into my finances and I think I finally have a real plan.

Here are the current balances you asked for:
- Capital One Quicksilver (...6144): $5,810
- Chase Freedom (...2387): $2,190

The plan I'm working with:
- Cutting ~$40/mo in subscriptions (dropped Game Pass, Disney+, YouTube Premium)
- Keeping dining to $75/mo (down from some bigger months)
- Throwing $823/mo total at the cards — killing Chase first (should be done by ~September), then redirecting everything to Capital One
- Building my emergency savings to $1,500 before going all-in on debt with the Stripe income
- Targeting debt-free by spring 2027

I'm also going to look into the rent increase — it jumped $50/mo in March without the required 45-day notice, so I'm going to ask management about it.

Would love to schedule a call to walk through the updated timeline with you. I'm flexible most evenings after 5.

Thanks for everything — the progress is starting to feel real.

Daniel
